understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to position a pushable block or object onto a goal tile (marked with '+' in the semantic grid). The level would report WIN when this success condition was met. The puzzle required strategic positioning of objects through a series of moves within a limited step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The game world used pixel coordinates [x, y] on a 3-pixels-per-cell grid. Multiple selectable pieces could be controlled by clicking (CLICK with target_position) to switch between them. Movement actions (MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN) moved the currently selected piece in that direction. Pushable objects could be shifted if a selected piece moved into them from an adjacent cell. Walls and obstacles could block or restrict movement in various ways. The level had a finite step budget (56+ steps available initially); once exhausted, the level ended regardless of completion status. In this run, the step budget was depleted before achieving WIN, suggesting the puzzle solution required more efficient pathfinding or a different sequence of moves/selections.
